pattern: Slide a fixed 3x3 window over every interior anchor and sum the seven hourglass cells, keeping the running best.
hints: The hourglass shape is rigid: pick where its top-left corner sits and the other six cells follow.
Only anchors where a full 3x3 block fits inside the grid can host an hourglass, so bound your loops accordingly.
For each top-left (r, c) with r+2 and c+2 in bounds, add the top row, the single middle cell, and the bottom row, then track the maximum.

edge_inputs.description: minimum 3x3 grid has exactly one hourglass
all zeros gives a zero maximum
the maximum hourglass is not the top-left one
large middle cell does not always win

stress.description: random rectangular grid with small bounds covering many overlapping hourglasses
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(3, max(3, size))
    n = rng.randint(3, max(3, size))
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 1000000) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: varying dimensions exercise the boundary of the sliding window
